content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322·5662 
John H. Larson 
County Counsel 
County of Los Angeles 
Administration 
322·5660 
648 Hall of Administration 
Los Angeles, CA 
90012 
Dear Mr. Larson: 
Executive/legal 
3n·5901 
Enforcement 
322·6441 
October 31, 1983 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322·6444 
Re: 
Gift Reporting, Disquali-
fication -
Los Angeles County 
Supervisors Dana, Schabarum 
and Antonovich 
Advice Letter No. A-83-245 
Reference is made to your letters, dated May 27, 1983, to 
the above-referenced supervisors. 
In response to an inquiry 
from Roger Brown of our Enforcement Division, as to their filing 
amended Statements of Economic Interests (which disclosed 
reduced values for certain gifts), these three supervisors 
forwarded to us copies of your letters advising them that they 
could "pay down" the value of the gift ~ post facto. 
This 
advice was in error. 
We have sent each supervisor a letter (copy enclosed) 
explaining that any payment made to reduce the value of a gift 
must be made within 30 days of receipt of the gift. 
Clearly 
this did not occur in these cases. 
Consequently, if the correct 
value of any gifts received was $250 or more, then 
disqualification would be required in the appropriate 
circumstances. 
Obviously, by now the 12-month disqualification 
period will already have expired as to gifts received in the 
first 9-1/2 months of 1982. 
Hopefully for gifts received in 
1983 appropriate steps will already have been taken to avoid any 
similar future difficulties. 


John H. Larson 
October 31, 1983 
Page 2 
Should you have any questions regarding this matter, please 
do not hesitate to contact me. 
I may be reached at 
(916) 322-5901. 
REL:plh 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Robert E. Leidigh 
Counsel 
Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: May county supervisors reduce the reported value of gifts received by making payments after the fact (post facto) and filing amended Statements of Economic Interest?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No. Any payment to reduce the value of a gift must be made within 30 days of receipt of the gift. If the correct gift value is $250 or more, disqualification is required in appropriate circumstances, and the 12-month disqualification period begins from the date of receipt, not from the date of amended filing.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains clear advisory opinion responding to County Counsel's erroneous guidance regarding gift valuation and disqualification procedures. The response explains the error and provides correct interpretation of FPPC rules.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May county supervisors reduce the reported value of gifts received by making payments after the fact (post facto) and filing amended Statements of Economic Interest?

CONCLUSION: No. Any payment to reduce the value of a gift must be made within 30 days of receipt of the gift. If the correct gift value is $250 or more, disqualification is required in appropriate circumstances, and the 12-month disqualification period begins from the date of receipt, not from the date of amended filing.

In response to an inquiry from Roger Brown of our Enforcement Division, as to their filing amended Statements of Economic Interests (which disclosed reduced values for certain gifts), these three supervisors forwarded to us copies of your letters advising them that they could "pay down" the value of the gift ~ post facto. This advice was in error. We have sent each supervisor a letter (copy enclosed) explaining that any payment made to reduce the value of a gift must be made within 30 days of receipt of the gift. Clearly this did not occur in these cases. Consequently, if the correct value of any gifts received was $250 or more, then disqualification would be required in the appropriate circumstances. Obviously, by now the 12-month disqualification period will already have expired as to gifts received in the first 9-1/2 months of 1982. Hopefully for gifts received in 1983 appropriate steps will already have been taken to avoid any similar future difficulties. John H. Leidigh Counsel Legal Division
embedding.summary: FPPC corrects erroneous legal advice given by Los Angeles County Counsel to three supervisors, clarifying that gifts cannot be 'paid down' after receipt and that proper gift valuation and disqualification requirements must be followed within statutory timeframes.
